tweets.text: "There's so much work to do just on the finance side, and when you look at where the product market fit is today, it's really in upgrading finance" Jesse on why the social bet didn't work out and where the product market fit actually is "Some of it was just the timing of it. If you look at where crypto is getting product market fit today, it's really in finance, stablecoins, tokenization, prediction markets, perps, these categories are growing incredibly quickly" "One of the theses I've always had is that the whole world is going to be consumed by crypto. We're basically going to go from the bare bones crypto of Bitcoin, up to having smart contracts, to building finance, to every single part of our society leveraging this new technology to make it more efficient, more fair, more free, and more global" "That of course includes social. But the thing that maybe went wrong there was there's so much work to do just on the finance side"
tweets.summary: Coinbase's Jesse Pollak says crypto's product-market fit is in finance: stablecoins, tokenization, prediction markets, and perps.

tweets.text: "There's always going to be oscillation in activity, particularly when you have new ecosystems cropping up and these kind of meadows emerging" Jesse responds to Robinhood Chain passing Base on daily active users and fees "When you look at Base today, we've been building for three years. We have the largest spot onchain markets in crypto, a ton of stablecoin volume, and an incredibly dense network of builders that have made Base their home" "What we're focused on is the long term. Making sure Base is fast, trusted, easy to use, the place where developers come to build, and expanding market types so people can trade spot, perps, predictions, borrow and lend, all in one place" "We look at folks coming into the ecosystem as positive validation of our strategy. The largest financial companies in the world are saying let's bring our products onchain, and that's just growing the pie"
tweets.summary: Jesse Pollak on Base's onchain markets, stablecoin volume, and expanding to spot, perps, predictions, and lending amid Robinhood Chain rivalry.

tweets.text: "The big reflection for me over the last six months is that building Base into the blockchain for global finance ... is just a massive undertaking and having my full focus on that is required to achieve all that we want to achieve there," @jessepollak on why it was necessary to hand over control of Base app to Cobie https://t.co/QIidlPO3ts
tweets.summary: Jesse Pollak hands Base app control to Cobie to focus on building Base into the blockchain for global finance.

tweets.text: "We look at that as a really positive validation of our strategy," @jessepollak on competition from Robinhood Chain https://t.co/qFUIgrzQTf

tweets.text: "There's always going to be oscillation in activity and oscillation in prices, particularly when you have new ecosystems cropping up ... What we’re focused on is the long term" @jessepollak on competition from Robinhood Chain https://t.co/uWS4B7VfpG
tweets.summary: Base lead downplays price/activity swings amid Robinhood Chain competition, stressing long-term focus on the L2 ecosystem.

That’s not what we want though, or what we have worked so hard for the last 10 years to achieve. In the long run, a well-functioning market will lift all boats. Let’s get CLARITY done!!

tweets.text: SENATOR LUMMIS @SenLummis IS 💯 ON 🎯 I represent (pro-bono) almost 4K thousand investors in the case of @linqtoinc - currently in Bankruptcy Court. Although Linqto doesn’t involve individual crypto tokens it does involve pre-IPO shares of Crypto Companies, including @Ripple, @krakenfx, @UpholdInc and others. Linqto is a fraud case, including deceptive marketing tactics, undisclosed excessive mark-ups, misleading, false advertising, etc. Investors believed they owned pre-IPO shares of companies. Linqto investors believed they owned the shares because they were told so. More importantly, investors were told, in writing, that the securities they owned were in fact “bankruptcy-proof” - because customer shares were segregated. Just as in FTX, Celsius and other cases, the VERY FIRST ISSUE in bankruptcy court is who owns the customer accounts (shares, tokens, cash, etc). The issue becomes was there segregation in fact, not just in name. Courts look at whether the funds were actually kept separate - not commingled with operating funds - regardless of what the account was called. This is the exact issue that sank customers in FTX and Celsius. The documentation said “custodial,” but the money was commingled and used, so courts treated it as ESTATE PROPERTY subject to the general unsecured creditor pool. In Linqto, I immediately filed a a motion in Bankruptcy Court asking the judge to impose a Constructive Trust - ruling that customers owned the shares and that they were held in trust. Although the Creditors Committee, Debtor (Linqto) and Deaton Represented Creditors have settled the issue, Customers have lost tens of millions of dollars related to their investments. I may work pro-bono but bankruptcy lawyers and other professionals (accountants, auditors, financial advisors, etc), do not. The median hourly fee for each professional is $1,200 per hour, with some charging as high as $2,500 per hour. In FTX alone, Sullivan & Cromwell billed for $360 million with $254 million approved by the Court. THe FTX bankruptcy estate paid lawyers and restructuring advisers nearly $1 BILLION in total fees and expenses, per the January 2026 post-confirmation report. WE NEED CLARITY!
tweets.summary: Linqto bankruptcy/fraud case over pre-IPO shares of crypto firms raises custody-segregation ownership disputes akin to FTX and Celsius.

tweets.text: 🚨NEW: The @USHCC, which says it represents millions of Hispanic-owned businesses across the U.S., sent a letter to Senate leaders this week saying it shares community banks’ concerns that the Clarity Act could accelerate deposit flight. The group argues the bill would reduce lending to Hispanic-owned small businesses and weaken investment in underserved communities.
tweets.summary: USHCC warns Senate the Clarity Act could accelerate deposit flight and reduce lending to Hispanic-owned small businesses.

tweets.text: RT @0xYager: If I was on the Hill this week advocating for Clarity I would be hammering the Cornyn & Cortez Masto quotes... "Crypto is not going to be loaning any money for small businesses." This couldn't be farther from the truth... 1/ This demonstrates a misunderstanding of what crypto is -- it is not a separate financial ecosystem for only crypto users. It is an upgrade to the system, driving costs lower and increasing accessibility. This is a bill for all Americans, not just crypto users. 2/ Small businesses face increasingly prohibitive and extractive options to access capital. Usury rates through the roof, interest rates climbing, Big Banks consolidating regional banks faster than ever. Small businesses are looking to Congress to create a federal regulatory framework so they can feel comfortable accessing lending opportunities from crypto providers. 3/ Crypto is innovating while institutions squeeze out SMEs. Take Perps for example, tick sizes and risk management are prohibitive barriers to entry for any business but the largest to properly hedge risk across international events/economics. Take a Texas truck stop operator looking to hedge fuel costs across the Iran conflict. They can't onboard to an FCM, they can't access the CME. But they can trade Perps at tick sizes that make sense for them! 4/ If crypto right now was taking an increasing share of small business lending, it would be shouted from the mountain tops about how crypto is crushing community lending and creating systemic risk. What is needed are federal regulations that protect consumers and small businesses, that can help bridge the divide between crypto and traditional lending so that a small business is not choosing one or the other, a community bank is not left out of the picture, but instead they are choosing the types of products to offer -- of which crypto lending is a new category with new efficiencies and compliance mechanisms that can enhance lending operations.
tweets.summary: Advocacy thread urging passage of the Clarity Act, arguing crypto lending and perps expand capital access for small businesses.

tweets.text: Democrat opposition to the historic ethics provision in the Clarity Act appears to take one of two forms, either: (1) An ethics provision that lacks enforcement by state AGs is meaningless. (2) An ethics provision that does not penalize President Trump for prior crypto activity is unsatisfactory. If you hold position (1), then you are basically saying that ALL current federal ethics laws are meaningless because none of them are enforceable by state AGs. If you hold position (2), then there is literally nothing that can be done to appease you because what you are advocating for is blatantly unconstitutional (see Article I, Section 9).
tweets.summary: Rebuts Democratic objections to the CLARITY Act's crypto ethics provision on enforcement and constitutional grounds.

tweets.text: RT @brian_armstrong: The Clarity Act is ready for a full Senate floor vote. The bill represents a true bipartisan compromise with thousands of hours of work on both sides, and it couldn't come at a better time. The status quo in the U.S. isn't working. There's no federal framework, so bad actors like FTX can harm U.S. customers and much of the industry has gone offshore totally outside U.S. purview. This bill fixes that with strong consumer protections, real tools for law enforcement, and a path for America to lead in this industry. Crypto can't be uninvented at this point, so whether you love crypto or hate crypto, you should want clear federal laws. And it's also simply good politics: 70% of American voters say the U.S. should have already passed comprehensive crypto legislation. Let's finish this! Special thanks to the @standwithcrypto members who sent 950k contacts to their representatives in congress. Keep it up.
tweets.summary: Armstrong urges a full Senate floor vote on the bipartisan CLARITY Act to create a US federal crypto framework.

tweets.text: Hey @Sen_Alsobrooks - along with every Democrat who argues they can’t vote for the Clarity Act unless state Attorneys General are in charge of the ethics enforcement: You’re suggesting is what’s unserious. I’ve never been shy about criticizing this President when I think he’s gotten it wrong, including on crypto policy, where I’ve pushed back on positions I believed were bad for retail investors and market integrity. But on this specific question, the Democrats’ insistence on state Attorneys General enforcement doesn’t hold up. The CLARITY Act is federal legislation establishing a federal regulatory framework for digital assets. The Department of Justice - not fifty different state AGs with fifty different political incentives and fifty different interpretations - is the appropriate body to enforce federal law. Handing enforcement to state AGs invites exactly the kind of inconsistent, patchwork application that makes it harder for legitimate market participants to know what the rules actually are, while opening the door to the kind of politicized targeting we’ve already seen state AGs use against industries and individuals they simply disagree with. If Democrats are serious about consumer protection in crypto markets, they should want uniform, predictable federal enforcement - not a system where enforcement varies depending on which state you’re in and which party controls that AG’s office. Currently, there are no restrictions on government officials issuing meme coins, NFTs, or other cryptocurrencies. FTX happened in part because of the lack of regulatory clarity in America and retail investors lost big time going offshore. Make no mistake about it: voting no comes down to not wanting Trump to take a legislative victory lap shortly before the midterms. Period. And it’s disgusting, un-patriotic and hurts the people you’re claiming to want to protect.
tweets.summary: Deaton argues CLARITY Act crypto enforcement belongs with DOJ, not state AGs, favoring uniform federal digital asset rules.

tweets.text: This is the strongest version of the bill yet — bipartisan, pro-consumer, and ready to go. Bring it to a vote and pass the CLARITY Act.
tweets.summary: Standwithcrypto urges Congress to pass the CLARITY Act, calling it a bipartisan, pro-consumer crypto market structure bill.

tweets.text: What surprised me on @bitsandbips: @AlexWilsonTGB argues PayPal 'chose to fail' at stablecoins, and that Stripe's $53B bid for it is really a bet on distribution nobody else built. 💳 Worth a listen. Timestamps: 📣 01:33 Cape: Get 33% off your first six months of America's privacy first mobile carrier at https://t.co/2ZtLziXsHV 🤝 02:49 Meet Alex Wilson: the Cyclops founder on his second run at stablecoin payments 💳 04:59 Stripe's $53B bid for PayPal and what it says about the distribution battle ⚔️ 16:51 The stablecoin distribution war: Circle, the bank-led OUSD, and Robinhood's USDG 🚀 25:47 SpaceX's post-IPO round trip and what record US equity issuance signals 🇨🇳 32:56 Kimi K3: China's cheap open weight model rattles US AI and chip stocks 🧩 38:42 Where Ram thinks AI's value actually accrues: semis, then the app layer 🧠 41:23 Austin on AI in health care and Claude Fable 5's Jacobian conjecture breakthrough 🛡️ 48:11 Why Austin says there's no way to opt out of the AI guardrails race ⚖️ 52:12 The Clarity Act: why Chris says it's one shot on goal before recess
tweets.summary: Podcast covers the stablecoin distribution war (Circle, OUSD, Robinhood's USDG) and the Clarity Act's path to a vote.

tweets.text: Great meeting with @SenAshleyMoody to discuss the importance of well-functioning commodity derivatives markets to the farmers, ranchers, and producers in our home state of Florida. We also discussed the importance of regulatory CLARITY for crypto asset markets to ensure the new frontier of finance is built in the United States.

tweets.text: RT @CJRobins01: @HesterPeirce 3/ We met with the SEC Crypto Task Force yesterday to share our thoughts on these issues. We are excited to continue engaging and to be an active steward for both the @morpho protocol and the crypto industry.
tweets.summary: Morpho protocol counsel met with SEC Crypto Task Force to discuss DeFi vault regulatory treatment.

tweets.text: RT @CJRobins01: @HesterPeirce 2/ Vaults represent a significant opportunity for crypto to update the financial systems with open, permissionless infrastructure. As Commissioner Peirce notes "[v]aults are not uniform", they operate on spectrum, which requires careful consideration of vault design and activity.
tweets.summary: Advocates argue DeFi vaults need tailored SEC treatment, citing Commissioner Peirce's view that vaults are non-uniform.

tweets.text: RT @iampaulgrewal: The SEC deleted a full year of Gary Gensler's communications at the peak of his campaign against crypto. The FDIC tried to secretly cut off an entire lawful industry from the banking system and buried the evidence. The US government doesn't get to operate like this. Unless the American people allow them to. So @Coinbase sued, won, and exposed the details, resulting in one of the largest FOIA awards in each agency's history. More details below.
tweets.summary: Coinbase's FOIA suit exposed SEC deletion of Gensler-era records and FDIC crypto debanking, yielding major FOIA awards.

tweets.text: RT @CJRobins01: 1/We thank @HesterPeirce and the Crypto Task Force staff for continuing to lead with thoughtful, open dialogue on issues critical to American innovation and capital formation.
tweets.summary: Praises SEC Commissioner Peirce and Crypto Task Force for open dialogue on crypto innovation and capital formation.

tweets.summary: Sen. Lummis releases the Clarity Act, comprehensive crypto market structure legislation targeting illicit finance and innovation.

tweets.text: RT @Policy_Solution: The ethics excuse just collapsed. President Trump agreed to strict rules covering every federal official, including himself. The Senate has no reason left to delay. Pass the Clarity Act.
tweets.summary: Advocates Senate passage of the crypto market structure Clarity Act, citing new federal ethics rules removing delay excuses.

tweets.text: 232,000 American jobs supported by the crypto industry. Across all 50 states. This is not a niche story. It is an economic one. The @NatCryptoAssoc’s latest research maps the full footprint.
tweets.summary: National Crypto Association research maps 232,000 US jobs supported by the crypto industry across all 50 states.

tweets.text: RT @fund_defi: The Senate Clarity Act text is out and the latest draft combines the Banking and Ag Committee titles passed out of the respective committees. We greatly appreciate the herculean bipartisan effort that went into getting us to this moment. As always, the DEF team reviews text with an eye toward how the bill will impact DeFi developers and users. Here are our thoughts so far: While no bill is perfect and significant compromises were made along the way to get here, the Clarity Act maintains important protections for DeFi developers. First, the BRCA is unchanged, as are developer protections under the Exchange Act (Sec. 10601) and the Commodity Exchange Act (Sec. 20209), and the self-custody provision (Sec. 10605, Keep Your Coins Act). While developer protections under the Exchange Act (Sec. 10601) reflect a hard fought compromise—with some protections for DeFi trading protocols, messaging systems, and self-custody hardware/software subject to rulemaking—protections under the CEA remain the same as the House-passed version of Clarity. Second, the bill includes 25 sections related to sanctions and AML, and as a result of law enforcement groups’ concerns, it now includes new Title IX, Law Enforcement Tools. At first glance, this title appears to be smart regulation that will provide law enforcement with appropriate tools without overregulating software developers. Title IX comes in addition to Title II, Protecting Against Illicit Finance, and Title III, Responsible Innovation in Decentralized Finance, which include tools for law enforcement and rulemakings that extend obligations under the Bank Secrecy Act to digital asset intermediaries. These titles were added in direct response to concerns that the bill needed to do more to address illicit finance and give law enforcement new tools and funding that would otherwise not exist. Next up? We head toward a few procedural motions before a final vote in the Senate. The DEF team will track amendments that affect DeFi and will share updates accordingly. To the Senators and staff who have dedicated countless hours drafting this bill, thank you. Your work is greatly appreciated, and we are excited to see this bill progress through Congress.
tweets.summary: DEF analyzes Senate Clarity Act text, noting preserved DeFi developer protections and new AML/law enforcement titles ahead of a Senate vote.

tweets.text: Our team was on Capitol Hill today for another congressional staff briefing on the Clarity Act. The discussion came at a timely moment as the Senate enters the final stretch of consideration. Thank you to @SolanaInstitute's @thecolinmclaren and @Coinbase's @robinrcook for joining BA's @lindsayfraser0 to discuss the bill's framework and answer questions from staff.
tweets.summary: Blockchain Association held a congressional staff briefing on the Clarity Act as the Senate enters final consideration.

tweets.text: The full text of the Clarity Act is out - the most comprehensive crypto market structure bill Congress has ever produced. Real consumer protections and the toughest illicit-finance tools crypto has ever had. Your voices got us here. But it's not law until it's signed. Keep pushing Congress to get it done. 👉 https://t.co/4gp0IRJmeU
tweets.summary: Full text of the Clarity Act, a comprehensive crypto market structure bill with consumer protections and illicit-finance tools, is released.

tweets.text: Years of work went into this text. It shows. Robust consumer protections. Tools for law enforcement. Certainty for developers. Substantive ethics requirements. This is a strong bill that secures American leadership in digital assets. Let's get this to the President's desk. 🇺🇸

tweets.text: History will remember this as the moment a president chose a higher standard of ethics than the law required of him. This agreement bans ALL federal officials — including the President — from issuing or sponsoring a digital asset for profit, with real enforcement and real penalties. Thank you, @POTUS! Let's get the Clarity Act passed NOW.
tweets.summary: Sen. Lummis touts CLARITY Act ethics deal barring federal officials, including the President, from issuing digital assets for profit.

tweets.text: The full text of the CLARITY Act is here. This is the most comprehensive crypto market structure bill Congress has ever produced. Bipartisan work delivered: ✅ One clear federal framework. ✅ Real protections for U.S crypto owners ✅ The toughest illicit-finance tools crypto has ever had Stand With Crypto advocates have been fighting for this, contacting their lawmakers 950K times for CLARITY, and the finish line is in sight. It’s time to pass this bill before the Senate leaves for recess.
tweets.summary: Stand With Crypto shares full CLARITY Act text, calling it the most comprehensive crypto market structure bill, urging Senate passage.

tweets.text: I want to thank my Democratic colleagues for their important contributions to this draft, and express my commitment to reaching a deal in the coming days that will allow this legislation to become law. Consumer protection and pro-innovation policy aren't opposites — this bill proves it.

tweets.text: Law enforcement: A new section of the bill is entirely dedicated to strengthening law enforcement’s ability to investigate crypto-related crime. 📌Increases funding for state and local crypto investigations and blockchain analytics tools. 📌Creates new training programs for law enforcement and prosecutors. 📌Establishes a “cyber center” to combat threats from nation-state actors like North Korea and Iran. 📌Creates a public-private task force to coordinate efforts against crypto fraud. 📌Requires stablecoin issuers to comply with lawful orders to freeze, seize, burn, and reissue tokens when appropriate.
tweets.summary: New bill section boosts crypto crime enforcement funding, cyber center, and stablecoin freeze/seize compliance mandates.

tweets.text: 🚨NEW: Senate Republicans have just released an updated version of the Clarity Act following briefing calls with stakeholders this morning. Here are some of the key provisions in the latest text, including ethics, the BRCA and other areas we’ve been following closely.
tweets.summary: Senate Republicans release updated Clarity Act text with new ethics, BRCA and market structure provisions.
